title: farm build: push built images to registry
body:
Update farm build to directly push images to a registry
after all the builds are complete on all the nodes.
A manifest list is then created locally and pushed to
the registry as well.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Farm build pushes built images from the farm nodes directly to a registry. A manifest list is then created locally and pushed to the registry as well.
```

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [umohnani8] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Cockpit tests failed for commit 7d1691a148c5143939a588609905ac8944e12922. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.

>         # mkdir: cannot create directory ‘/auth’: Permission denied

Relevant code is `mkdir -p $AUTHDIR`, where `AUTHDIR=${PODMAN_LOGIN_WORKDIR}/auth` and `PODMAN_LOGIN_WORKDIR` is defined in `test/system/setup_suite.bash` but not in your `test/farm` copy.

Solution: copy the `PODMAN_LOGIN_WORKDIR` definition into your `test/farm/setup_suite.bash`.

Caveat: that's only the proximate cause. There may be other problems down the line.

Thanks @edsantiago, that helped fix the tests!

Tests are green, @rhatdan @nalind @mheon PTAL
LGTM
LGTM once @edsantiago 's concnern is addressed.
LGTM once comments addressed
At this point, are the `PushToRegistry()` methods saving their caller any work?

> At this point, are the `PushToRegistry()` methods saving their caller any work?

@nalind what do you mean? `PushToRegistry()` directly pushes the images built on the various farm nodes to the registry specified by the user. We are no longer copying the images from the farm nodes to the local machine. What work should it be saving its caller from?

> > At this point, are the `PushToRegistry()` methods saving their caller any work?
> 
> @nalind what do you mean? `PushToRegistry()` directly pushes the images built on the various farm nodes to the registry specified by the user. We are no longer copying the images from the farm nodes to the local machine. What work should it be saving its caller from?

`PushToRegistry()`'s caller already has to build an options struct to call `PushToRegistry()`, and having it instead build an options struct for `Push()` and call it instead doesn't appear to be any more complicated.  At that point we wouldn't need `PushToRegistry()` at all.

> > > At this point, are the `PushToRegistry()` methods saving their caller any work?
> > 
> > 
> > @nalind what do you mean? `PushToRegistry()` directly pushes the images built on the various farm nodes to the registry specified by the user. We are no longer copying the images from the farm nodes to the local machine. What work should it be saving its caller from?
> 
> `PushToRegistry()`'s caller already has to build an options struct to call `PushToRegistry()`, and having it instead build an options struct for `Push()` and call it instead doesn't appear to be any more complicated. At that point we wouldn't need `PushToRegistry()` at all.

Ah right, that makes sense. Fixed it up to directly call `Push()` now!
@mheon @rhatdan @nalind this is ready for merge, PTAL
I'd like a final approval from @nalind before I push the button, but LGTM

I'm still not in favor of having custom retrying logic in `pkg/farm.listLocal.build()`.  We have a `github.com/containers/common/pkg/retry.IfNecessary()` for doing that, and if the call to that down in `github.com/containers/common/libimage.copier.copy()` isn't getting triggered, my preference would be to correct that.

@nalind to unblock the PR, I have removed the retry logic in list_builder.go.
 
I will look into if and why the retry logic in c/image is not being triggered and fix if needed in a follow up PR.
LGTM, then, pending tests passing.
/lgtm

/hold
Unless we're skipping that failing test?
Machine mac is, for now, an expected/allowed failure. Still working on getting OS X CI passing
/cherry-pick v4.9
